sources.notes: Statement and constraints: two binary strings define an n*n XOR grid; sum minimum flips over all endpoints.
Official solution confirms each endpoint cost can be written as min(f(x,y),g(x,y)) and optimized with a min/absolute-value identity.
Independent notes confirm aggregating all (x,y) by prefix counts of zeroes and ones.

annotation.summary: Binary strings a,b generate grid value a_i xor b_j. For every endpoint (x,y), compute the minimum flips needed so there is an all-zero monotone path from (1,1) to (x,y), and sum over all endpoints.
annotation.constraints: sum n <= 200000, so n^2 endpoints cannot be enumerated explicitly; prefix counts and sorting/bucketing are needed.
annotation.core_idea: A monotone path to (x,y) connects the first x rows and first y columns, so those row/column bits must be made consistent. Let prefix counts of zeroes/ones in a[1..x] and b[1..y] be A0,A1,B0,B1. The endpoint cost is min(A1+B1, A0+B0). Convert min to (sum - abs(diff))/2 or an equivalent threshold formula, then aggregate all pairs of one-dimensional prefix differences using sorting/prefix sums or buckets.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) with sorting, or O(n) with bounded-difference buckets
tricks: Path connectivity turns into prefix row/column same-color constraints.

solution_variants.name: prefix-difference-absolute-sum
solution_variants.summary: Compute prefix zero/one counts and differences for a and b, rewrite each endpoint cost as a min/absolute-value expression, and aggregate all pairs by sorting or buckets.

solution_variants.name: two-pointer-sorted-prefixes
solution_variants.summary: Build one-dimensional values from each prefix, sort them, and maintain prefix sums with a two-pointer threshold to sum all min/absolute contributions.

tags.evidence: Each endpoint's cost is determined only by prefix counts in a[1..x] and b[1..y].
The core optimization uses min(f,g)=(f+g-|f-g|)/2 or an equivalent threshold identity to collapse a 2D sum into 1D aggregation.
Sorting prefix differences allows all pair absolute/min contributions to be accumulated linearly.
With bounded difference range, prefix zero/one differences can be bucketed instead of enumerating all endpoints.
